# Philo — Scaffold Summary

**Generated by Freebuff Desktop** | July 17, 2026

---

## Project Overview

Full-stack Next.js 15 application (App Router, TypeScript strict) with:
- Supabase (Postgres + Auth + pgvector + Storage + Realtime)
- OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini + text-embedding-3-small)
- Stripe (subscriptions + customer portal)
- Deepgram Nova-3 (voice transcription)
- shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS (editorial/scholarly theme)
- Deploy target: Vercel

---

```
│   ├── voice.ts                      # Voice note upload + transcription
│   └── auth.ts                       # Session user + signOut helpers
├── supabase/migrations/
│   ├── 0001_init_extensions.sql      # pgvector, citext, pg_trgm, updated_at trigger
│   ├── 0002_profiles_and_auth.sql    # profiles table, handle_new_user trigger
│   ├── 0003_passages_and_corpus.sql  # philosophers, works, chapters, passages, relations
│   ├── 0004_user_annotations.sql     # user_notes, user_highlights, voice_notes
│   ├── 0005_economy.sql              # energy_charges, subscriptions, usage_events
│   ├── 0006_debate.sql               # matches, judges_log, leaderboards, weekly view
│   ├── 0007_rls_policies.sql         # RLS on all user-owned tables
│   ├── 0008_seed_metadata.sql        # Aquinas corpus skeleton (metadata only)

├── types/

├── tailwind.config.js                # Editorial theme (parchment + scholar palette)

├── tsconfig.json                     # Strict mode, bundler resolution
├── next.config.ts                    # Supabase image domains, 10mb server actions
└── package.json                      # All dependencies pinned
```

---

## Supabase Migrations (run in order)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `0001_init_extensions.sql` | Enable pgvector, citext, pg_trgm; create `updated_at` trigger function |
| `0002_profiles_and_auth.sql` | `profiles` table extending `auth.users`; auto-create on signup |
| `0003_passages_and_corpus.sql` | `philosophers`, `works`, `chapters`, `passages` (with pgvector index), `philosopher_relations` |
| `0004_user_annotations.sql` | `user_notes`, `user_highlights`, `voice_notes` tables |
| `0005_economy.sql` | `energy_charges`, `subscriptions`, `usage_events` tables |
| `0006_debate.sql` | `matches`, `judges_log`, `leaderboards`, `weekly_leaderboard` view |
| `0007_rls_policies.sql` | Row-level security policies for every user-owned table |
| `0008_seed_metadata.sql` | Saint Thomas Aquinas philosopher + works + chapter skeletons (no passage text) |
| `0009_match_passages_rpc.sql` | Unique constraints for upserts + `match_passages` pgvector RPC + storage bucket note |

**IMPORTANT:** After running migrations, also create the storage bucket manually:
```sql
SELECT storage.create_bucket('voice_notes', '{"public": false}');
```

## Pre-Flight Commands (all passed ✓)

```bash
# Run from C:\dev\Philo
npm install                    # ✓ 558 packages
npx tsc --noEmit              # ✓ 0 errors
npx next build                 # ✓ 18 routes compiled
```

---

## Run Locally

```bash
cd C:\dev\Philo

# 1. Install dependencies
npm install

# 2. Copy environment template
copy .env.example .env.local

# 3. Fill in .env.local with your keys:
#    - Supabase URL, anon key, service role key
#    - OpenAI API key
#    - Stripe secret, publishable, webhook secret, price IDs
#    - Deepgram API key
#    - (Thresholds have defaults — only set to override)

# 4. Run the dev server
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) in your browser.

---

## Deploy to Vercel

1. Push to GitHub:
   ```powershell
   git add .
   git commit -m "Philo initial scaffold"
   git push -u origin main
   ```

2. Import the repo in Vercel dashboard → **Add New Project**.

3. In Vercel project settings → **Environment Variables**, paste all vars from `.env.example` with your real values.

4. Deploy. Vercel auto-detects Next.js.

5. Set up the Stripe webhook endpoint: `https://<your-domain>/api/webhook/stripe`

---

## Architecture Notes

- **Greek suffix routing:** `/philo/sophy` is the only active sub-namespace. `[suffix]` catch-all routes exist for future `theo`, `logic`, etc.
- **RAG pipeline:** User question → `text-embedding-3-small` → pgvector `match_passages` RPC (cosine similarity) → top 8 passages → `gpt-4o-mini` with system prompt → Zod-validated JSON response. Refusal gate: cosine < threshold → editorial refusal.
- **Energy economy:** Base charges + streak bonuses + soft cap. Configurable via env. Premium uncaps.
- **Debate judging:** 5 AI personas score each section; primary persona gets configurable weight multiplier.
- **Passage anchors:** Format `{slug}:{book_id}:{chapter_id}:{offset}` — stable forever for note/highlight binding.
- **Security:** RLS on all user tables, service role only in server-side lib and `/api/admin/ingest`, Stripe webhook signature verification, rate limiting with configurable windows.
